questions.question: Which class is most convenient for reading space-separated integers from a text file?
questions.explanation: Scanner tokenizes input and provides nextInt(), making it ideal for space-separated numbers.

questions.question: What does try-with-resources ensure when used with a BufferedReader?
options: A) The file is closed automatically even if an exception occurs
B) The file path is automatically created if missing
C) The file is read entirely into memory
questions.explanation: try-with-resources closes all AutoCloseable resources at scope exit, success or failure.

questions.question: Which method reads all lines of a small text file into a List<String>?
questions.explanation: Files.readAllLines(Path) returns a List<String> with the file’s lines.

questions.question: What happens if you forget to close a PrintWriter?
options: A) Nothing; Java closes it instantly
B) Data may remain unflushed and be missing from the file
C) The file is deleted automatically
D) The program will not compile
questions.explanation: Buffered output might not be flushed, so some data may not reach the file.

questions.question: Which snippet correctly writes two lines to output.txt?
options: A) PrintWriter pw = new PrintWriter("output.txt"); pw.println("A"); pw.println("B"); pw.close();
B) FileWriter fw = new FileWriter("output.txt"); fw.println("A\nB"); fw.close();
questions.explanation: PrintWriter supports println; FileWriter has write but not println; other options are invalid.

questions.question: If a method opens a file with new FileReader("in.txt"), what must it do regarding exceptions?
questions.explanation: Constructing FileReader can throw FileNotFoundException, a subclass of IOException, which is checked.

questions.question: When using Scanner, nextLine() differs from next() because:
options: A) nextLine() reads until a space; next() reads a full line
B) nextLine() reads an entire line; next() reads the next token
C) Both read tokens; there is no difference
D) nextLine() trims whitespace tokens; next() does not
questions.explanation: nextLine() returns the remainder of the current line; next() returns the next delimited token.

questions.question: Relative path "data/input.txt" is resolved relative to:
options: C) The program’s current working directory
questions.explanation: Relative paths use the JVM’s working directory, which may differ from the project folder.

questions.question: For large files where memory is a concern, which approach is best?
options: B) Read line-by-line with BufferedReader in a loop
C) Read the entire file into a String with Files.readString
D) Copy to a new file using PrintWriter without reading
questions.explanation: Streaming line-by-line minimizes memory usage compared to loading the entire file.

questions.question: Which construction adds buffering to improve performance when reading text?
questions.explanation: BufferedReader wraps a Reader to buffer input and provide readLine().
